prompt:
Build me a Leitner-box spaced-repetition trainer I can drive from the shell. I read up on this, so the scheduling is non-negotiable: five boxes, review intervals in days are box 1 -> 1, box 2 -> 2, box 3 -> 4, box 4 -> 7, box 5 -> 15. A passed review moves the card up one box (box 5 stays box 5) and schedules it interval-of-the-NEW-box days after the review day; a failed review sends it straight back to box 1, due the next day. New cards start in box 1, due the day they were added.

Entry point `srs.py`, deck lives in a JSON file selected by `--deck FILE`. Because this runs in scripts and tests, every command takes a required `--today YYYY-MM-DD` instead of consulting the clock (missing or malformed --today, or a bad pass/fail word, is a usage error, exit 2). Commands:

    python3 srs.py --deck d.json add "ohm" "unit of resistance" --today 2026-07-01   (silent; duplicate fronts are an error, exit 1)
    python3 srs.py --deck d.json due --today 2026-07-01     (cards with due <= today, one front per line, lowest box first, then earliest due, then front A-Z; empty deck or nothing due prints nothing, exit 0, and must not create the deck file)
    python3 srs.py --deck d.json review "ohm" pass --today 2026-07-01   (prints the new state exactly like `ohm: box 2, due 2026-07-03`)
    python3 srs.py --deck d.json stats --today 2026-07-01   (six lines: `box N: count` for N=1..5 then `due: N`)

Strictness I care about: reviewing a card before it's due is a refusal — exit 1, deck file untouched. Reviewing a card that doesn't exist, exit 1. Reviewing late is fine and schedules from the actual review day. State must survive across separate invocations, obviously.

test_srs.py is the acceptance suite (hands off) — `python3 test_srs.py` has to pass. Please structure this as a few focused modules (CLI, scheduler rules, deck persistence), not one script.
